Q7 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct regarding engineering stress-strain curve of 
1. The necking region is the area between the ultimate point and rupture.
2. Material undergoes changes in its atomic and crystalline structure in the strain hardening region.
3. Young's modulus increases due to increase in temperature

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
By increasing temperature atomic thermal vibration increases which decreases the value of Young's modulus of elasticity (E)

Q8 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are incorrect regarding beam of uniform strength?
1. It is a beam in which extreme fiber stresses are same along the length of beam.
2. It can't be achieved by varying width of the beam along the length.
3. It is a beam of uniform strength which refers to uniform distribution of bending stress at all cross-sections of the beam.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
A beam of uniform strength can be achieved by varying depth or width along the length of beam.

Consider the following statements:
1. Statically determinate real beam has statically indeterminate conjugate beam.
2. Statically indeterminate real beam has unstable conjugate beams.
3. Deflection at a point in a real beam is numerically equal to the slope at corresponding point in conjugate beam.
4. Deflection of a point in real beam is numerically equal to bending moment at the corresponding point in the conjugate beam.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. The anti-elastic curvature in a beam is due to the Poisson's effect.
2. Even for a doubly-symmetric cross-sectional beam, shear centre may not coincide with the centroid.
3. When the load passes through shear center, then there will be no twisting of cross-section.
Which of the above statement(s) are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Due to Poisson's effect, longitudinal strain leads to lateral strain which will lead to anticlastic
curvature (having opposite curvature at a point) for an originally rectangular cross-section.
• For a doubly symmetric section, shear centre always coincides with the centroid of the section.

Q13 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding SFD and BMD:
1. If SF changes sign at a section, then BM is either maximum or minimum at that section
2. If loading is symmetrical about mid point of beam then BMD and SFD will also be perfectly symmetric about mid point of beam.
3. If BM changes sign at a section, then SF all that section is either maximum or minimum
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
1. If loading is symmetrical about mid point of beam then BMD will also be symmetric about mid point and SFD will be skew symmetric about the mid point of beam.
2. If SF changes sign at a section, then the BM is either minimum or maximum at that section.
3. If BM changes sign at a section nothing can be said about SF.

Q29 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which one of the following statements is true with regard to the flexibility method of analysis?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The method can be applied to structures with any degree of static indeterminacy, it is most
practical and preferred for structures with a low degree of indeterminacy. This is because the
number of compatibility equations to be solved increases with the degree of indeterminacy,
making the calculations complex.

1. Degree of static indeterminacy is one.
2. Strain energy developed in the member due to the horizontal force at any one support is equal to zero.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q31 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Displacement method of structural analysis is generally preferred over force method of structural analysis when:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The displacement method is best applied to structures with a high degree of static indeterminacy
and a low degree of kinematic indeterminacy. This is because the number of equilibrium equations
to solve is equal to the kinematic indeterminacy, and it is more practical to solve for a smaller
number of displacement unknowns than a large number of force unknowns.

Q34 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding cantilever method:
1. Points of contra-flexure are located at the mid points of all beam.
2. The resultant moment of total horizontal load acting above any horizontal plane passing through assumed internal hinge of column is resisted by the axial force generated in various columns.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Points of contraflexure are located at the midpoints of all beams and columns. This is a key
assumption in the cantilever method to make the indeterminate structure determinate.
• The cantilever method assumes the frame behaves like a vertical cantilever beam, where
columns act as the "fibers" and the axial stresses in the columns (which create the resisting
moment) are proportional to their distances from the structure's centroidal axis.

Q37 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The method of consistent deformation in structural analysis falls in the category of

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Force method: The method of consistent deformation, also known as the flexibility method,
is classified as a force method of structural analysis.
• In this method, the primary unknowns that are solved for are the redundant forces or
moments in a statically indeterminate structure.
• The analysis is based on ensuring the compatibility of deformations, meaning that the
displacements and rotations of the structure are consistent with the original support conditions.

Q38 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In compression member, the plate element may buckle locally before the entire member fails. To avoid this which of the following recommendations are made?
1. Thickness of members is taken in terms of width of compression element.
2. Length of compression element is increased.
3. Length of member is increased.
4. Length of outstand is decreased.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
To prevent local buckling of compression member, thickness of member should be according to its width ratio.

Q42 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Intermediate vertical stiffeners are provided in the plate girders to

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Preventing Web Buckling: The primary function of intermediate vertical stiffeners is to
prevent the thin web of a plate girder from buckling under diagonal compressive stresses caused by shear forces.
• These stiffeners divide the web into smaller, stiffer panels, which increases the web's critical
buckling stress and its overall resistance to shear buckling.

Q43 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding lug angle:
1. Wherever lug angles are used to connect an angle member, the whole area of the member shall be taken as effective.
2. Atleast two bolts shall be used for attaching the lug angle to the angle member.
3. For angle members, lug angle and their connection to the gusset should be capable of developing
strength not less than 40% in excess of the force in outstanding leg of the angle member.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The attachment of the lug angle to the gusset should be capable of developing 20% in excess of
the force in outstanding leg. The attachment of the lug angle to the angle member should be
capable of developing 40% in excess of that force

Q49 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The design of members of water tank by working stress method is based on

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Cracking Resistance: Water-retaining structures like tanks are primarily designed to prevent leakage, making the control of cracks a critical criterion. The working stress method limits the tensile stresses in the concrete and steel to ensure cracks are either avoided or kept to a minimal width
• Adequate Strength: The members must also be designed to have sufficient strength to resist the forces (hoop tension, bending, shear) from the contained water and other loads to prevent structural failure. This is a fundamental requirement of any structural design method.

Q50 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Pressure line concept is also known as stress concept.
2. A change in the external moments in the elastic range of prestressed concrete beam results in
a shift of the pressure line rather than an increase in the resultant force in the beam.
3. In prestressed concrete sections, the load carrying mechanism comprise of a constant force with changing lever arm.
4. In reinforced concrete sections, the load-carrying mechanism comprise of a changing force with a constant lever arm.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The "pressure line" is the path of the resultant force, and the “stress concept” is the method of
analysis that combines the effects of prestressing and external loads to determine the final stresses
in the concrete. The pressure line is a tool used within the stress concept method.

Q54 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Reinforcement that is no longer required for flexure beyond a certain section, shall however
be extended by d or whichever is greater, before being curtailed in all situations.
2. Not more than half of the total bars should be bent up at the cut-off point.
3. The shear capacity at cut-off point should atleast be 1.5 times the shear force at that section.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
As per IS : 456-2000,
(i) Clause 26.2.3.1, for curtailment, reinforcement shall extend beyond the point at which it is no
longer required to resist flexure for a distance equal to the effective depth of the member or
12 times the bar diameter whichever is greater except at simple supports or ends of cantilever.
(ii) Clause 26.2.3.2, the shear at cut off point does not exceed two-third of that permitted including
the shear strength of web reinforcement provided.

Q81 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding oxidation pond:
1. The boundary between aerobic and anaerobic zones is not stationary and it depends on climatic conditions.
2. Aerobic conditions are maintained in upper portion of pond by oxygen generated by algae.
3. The pond has symbiotic behaviour of waste stabilisation through algae on one hand and bacteria on the other.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• The boundary between aerobic and anaerobic zones is dynamic and can shift depending on
factors like water temperature, sunlight exposure, and the amount of organic matter present.
• Due to the oxygen generated by algae, the upper portion of the pond maintains aerobic conditions.
• In an oxidation pond, algae produces oxygen through photosynthesis, which is then utilized
by bacteria to decompose organic matter. This symbiotic relationship is crucial for the
stabilization of waste.

Q86 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which one of the following procedures is used for sampling of fire gas in a chimney for SPM?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Isokinetic sampling is a method used to collect flue gas samples from a chimney in a way that
ensures the sample is representative of the overall particle concentration. It does this by matching
the velocity of the sampling probe to the velocity of the gas stream in the chimney. This is crucial
for accurately capturing suspended particulate matter (SPM) as it prevents larger particles from
settling out or smaller particles from being swept away.

Q87 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding testing of chlorine residuals:
1. Orthotolidine test indicates faulty and increased residuals because of impurities such as iron, manganese.
2. DPD test uses of Palin's DPD reagent and this test is widely used in modern days.
3. Starch iodide test is cheap and hence generally used for testing public supplies.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Statement 3 is incorrect. The starch-iodide test is used, but it is generally less accurate for low levels of free chlorine compared to DPD, only measures total chlorine, and is not typically the standard method for routine testing of public water supplies.

Q92 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The sensitiveness of a bubble tube can be increased by
1. Increasing the length of bubble.
2. Increasing the viscosity of the liquid.
3. Increasing the internal radius of bubble tube.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Increasing the length of the bubble: A larger or longer bubble allows for a more pronounced movement for a given angular tilt, which increases sensitivity.
• Decreasing the viscosity of the liquid: Higher viscosity makes the bubble movement more sluggish, reducing responsiveness and practical sensitivity. Therefore, decreasing viscosity Increases sensitivity.
• Increasing the internal radius (or diameter) of the tube: A larger internal radius of curvature means that for the same angular deviation, the bubble travels a greater distance along the tube making it more sensitive.

Q95 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding the use of spiral transition curves in horizontal alignment of pavements:
1. The rate of change of centrifugal acceleration is consistent (smooth) in a spiral transition curve.
2. Calculation and field implementation of a spiral transition curve is easy.
3. The radius of a spiral transition curve changes smoothly from infinity to the radius of the circular curve.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• A spiral transition curve provides a gradual (linear) change in curvature.
• This results in a smooth and consistent change in centrifugal acceleration, improving comfort and safety.
• Spiral curves follow simple mathematical relations.
• Surveyors can easily set out the curve on the ground using standard procedures.
• The radius of the spiral begins as infinite (straight road).
• It gradually reduces until it reaches the radius of the circular curve, ensuring no sudden change in direction.

Which of the following minor instruments are used for setting out right angles in chain surveying?
1. Cross staff
2. Optical square
3. Prism square
4. Auto level

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Cross staff, Optical square and Prism square are all minor instruments used for setting out
right angles (90 degrees) to a chain line during chain surveying.
• The cross staff is the simplest instrument, while the optical square and prism square are more
modern, precise optic instruments that work on the principle of double reflection to ensure an accurate 90-degree angles.

Q109 Not checked

Not attempted

Pick out the correct statement for relief displacement in aerial photographs.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Relief displacement is the shift of an object's image on a photograph due to its elevation; for
objects higher than the reference surface, this shift is always in a direction away from the principal
point. Conversely, objects below the datum will show a negative, inward displacement.

Q117 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding flow of water in soil:
1. Seepage velocity will be comparatively more in fine grained soil.
2. Occurrence of capillary water results into apparent cohesion in cohesive soil and also develop contact pressure between soil.
3. Head loss will remain same in all layers of soil if flow takes place parallel to bed plane.
Which of the above statement(s) are not correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Seepage velocity will be comparatively less in fine grained soil, as porosity of fine grained soil is more for a particular volume of soil.
• Occurrence of capillary water results into apparent cohesion in cohesionless soil.

Q118 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding compaction of soil:
1. Brittle failure of soil occurs if compacted on dry side of optimum.
2. Chances of swelling of soil will be lesser on dry side of optimum.
3. Pore water pressure is more if soil is compacted on wet side of optimum.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The soil compacted on dry side of optimum have a steeper stress-strain curve which signifies
high modulus of elasticity.
• Such soils have brittle failure like dense sands or over consolidated clays.
• A soil compacted on dry side of the optimum water content has high water deficiency and
more random orientation of particles. Hence results in more swelling.
• The pore water pressure developed for the soil compacted on dry side of the optimum is
therefore lesser due to presence of lesser water content.

Q119 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

The unconfined compressive strength of the clay is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
 
Q120 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding the compressibility of soil:
1. Consolidation of soil occurs due to change in the total stress.
2. Compaction of soil is due to expulsion of water.
3. Over consolidated clay will have predominantly more cohesive strength than frictional strength.
4. When standard penetration test is performed in fine sands below water table, dilatancy correction is applied after overburden correction.
Which of the above statements is/are INCORRECT?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Consolidation of soil occurs due to change in effective stress of the soil.
Compaction of soil is due to expulsion of air from voids.

Q122 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. A sheet pile wall acts as a retaining wall.
2. Sheet pile wall is light in weight and flexible.
3. A bulk head is a sheet pile wall used in docks and harbours.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• A sheet pile wall is a type of retaining structure designed to retain soil, water, or other fill materials laterally, thus acting as a retaining wall.
• Sheet pile walls, especially when compared to bulky gravity retaining walls, are considered to be thin, relatively light, and flexible structures.
• A bulkhead is a specific term for a sheet pile retaining wall used along a waterfront in places like docks and harbours to provide a vertical wall for ships to tie up alongside.

Not attempted

Match List-I (Parameter) with List-II (Methods) and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:

 
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Stage is measured using a bubble gauge recorder, which records the water level (stage) in a stream.
• Discharge can be measured using the dilution method, which involves adding a tracer to the flow and measuring its concentration downstream to calculate the flow rate.
• Infiltration rate is a process described and calculated by Horton's method, which is a widely used hydrological model.
• Wind velocity is a common atmospheric variable measured with an anemometer.

Q126 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A flood wave with a known inflow hydrograph is routed through a large reservoir. The outflow hydrograph will have,

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
When a flood wave passes through a large reservoir, the storage effect of the reservoir causes the
flood peak to be reduced (attenuated). This process also causes the flow to be released over a
longer period, resulting in the hydrograph having an increased time base (enlarged or extended).

Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Q130 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consumptive use of water for a crop represents:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Consumptive use of water for a crop refers to the total amount of water removed from the soilplant
system and returned to the atmosphere during the crop growth period. This includes:
• Transpiration by the plant (water vapor released through leaves).
• Evaporation from the adjacent soil surface, plant surfaces (interception), and any nearby water bodies within the field.
• A minor quantity of water that is incorporated into the plant tissue for its metabolic activities and growth.

Q133 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding cross regulator:
1. It increases supply in the parent channel downstream
2. It increases supply in the off taking channel
3. It increases water depth in the parent channel, upstream
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Increasing supply in the offtaking channel: The regulator is placed downstream of the offtaking
channel's head regulator. By partially closing the cross regulator, the water level
upstream is raised (headed up), ensuring the off-taking channel receives its required supply,
especially during periods of low flow in the parent channel.
• Increasing water depth in the parent channel, upstream: The primary mechanism for feeding
the off-taking channel is by raising the water level in the upstream section of the parent channel.

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

(b)

Solution

(c)

 
 
Q136 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A shaft spillway is located :
 

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The key features of a shaft spillway (often called a "morning glory" or "bell mouth" spillway) are:
• The inlet structure, which is a funnel-shaped crest, sits within the reservoir near the dam.
• When the water level in the reservoir rises above the crest, water flows over the edges and down into a vertical shaft.
• At the bottom, the shaft connects to a horizontal tunnel or conduit that runs under the dam
or through the dam's foundation to discharge the water downstream.

3. To support the dam.

Correct answer: A

(a)

Each of the next Thirteen (13) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): High COD to BOD ratio implies that waste sample has low biodegradability.
Statement (II): COD is a measure of total organic and inorganics matter while BOD is a measure of organic matter only.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
COD is the measure of total organic (biodegradable and non-biodegradable) matter while BOD is the measure of organic biodegradable matter only.

Statement (I): The check dames are constructed away from the confluence points.
Statement (II): Construction of check dam helps in flood control.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

 

Statement (I): For a large water surface, such as lake, evaporation rate always increases with the increase in depth of water in lake.
Statement (II): In winter season, the temperature of surrounding air is less while it is high for water in a deep lake therefore evaporation will increase, while for shallow lake in winter it will decrease.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Evaporation rate decreases with increase in depth of water for large water surface bodies in summer season.

Q141 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next Thirteen (13) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

 

Statement (I): Bernoulli’s equation is applicable to all the points in the flow field in the flow is irrotational.
Statement (II): The value of constant in the equation is different for different stream lines if the flow is rotational.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• For irrotational flows, Bernoulli’s equation can be applied along the stream lines as well as across the stream lines i.e. it can be applied any where in the flow.
• For rotational flows, Bernoulli’s equation can be applied only along the stream line, but not
across the stream lines. This is because the value of constant is different for every stream line.

(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

 

Statement (I): A beam of circular section is stronger in shear compared to one of rectangular section having same cross-sectional area.
Statement (II): For equal cross-sectional areas and shearing forces, the maximum intensity of shear stress in beam of circular cross-section is smaller than that for the rectangular section.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Circular bar is more stronger as there is no possibility of distortion in circular bar and maximum shear stress is uniform at outer fibre and magnitude wise less than rectangular bar.

Statement (II): Critical path method is deterministic in nature.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Scientific projects like launching of satellites are probabilistic in nature and hence PERT is preferred over CPM in such projects.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

heals the cracks itself.
